quiz.title: SAT Mathematics: Unit 9 Quiz 6
quiz.subtitle: Data Distributions
quiz.subject: SAT Preparation
quiz.topic: Shape, spread, and interpretation of distributions

questions.question: A dot plot shows a distribution that is heavily skewed to the left. Which of the following inequalities correctly describes the relationship between the mean and the median of this distribution?
options: Mean < Median
Mean > Median
Mean = Median
Standard Deviation < Mean
questions.explanation: In a left-skewed distribution, the 'tail' extends toward the lower values. These lower outliers pull the mean down more significantly than they affect the median, resulting in a mean that is less than the median.

questions.question:
The table below summarizes two separate distributions, $X$ and $Y$.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|}\hline \text{Distribution} & \text{Range} & \text{Mean} \\ \hline X & 50 & 100 \\ \hline Y & 50 & 100 \\ \hline\end{array}$$

If the values in distribution $X$ are mostly clustered near the mean, while the values in distribution $Y$ are mostly clustered near the minimum and maximum values, which of the following must be true?
options: The median of $X$ is greater than the median of $Y$.
The standard deviation of $X$ is less than the standard deviation of $Y$.
The standard deviation of $X$ is greater than the standard deviation of $Y$.
The total number of data points in $Y$ is greater than in $X$.
questions.explanation: Standard deviation measures the average distance of data points from the mean. Since distribution $X$ is tightly clustered around the mean and distribution $Y$ is spread out toward the extremes, distribution $X$ has a lower standard deviation.

questions.question:
The frequency table below shows a symmetric, bell-shaped distribution of heights for 100 students.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|}\hline \text{Statistic} & \text{Value} \\ \hline \text{Mean Height} & 65 \text{ inches} \\ \hline \text{Distribution Shape} & \text{Symmetric (Bell-Shaped)} \\ \hline\end{array}$$

If the mean height is 65 inches, which of the following is the best estimate for the median height of the students?
options: $55$ inches
$60$ inches
$65$ inches
$70$ inches
questions.explanation: In a perfectly symmetric distribution (like a bell curve), the mean, median, and mode are all located at the same center point. Therefore, the median is approximately equal to the mean of 65 inches.

questions.question: A histogram displays the scores on a difficult exam where most students scored between $40$ and $60$, but a few students scored between $90$ and $100$. Which of the following terms best describes the shape of this distribution?
options: Symmetric
Uniform
Skewed to the left
Skewed to the right
questions.explanation: Because the majority of the data is on the left (lower scores) and the 'tail' (the few high scores) extends to the right, the distribution is skewed to the right.

questions.question:
The table below compares the distribution of ages in two cities.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|}\hline \text{City} & \text{Median Age} & \text{IQR} \\ \hline \text{A} & 32 & 8 \\ \hline \text{B} & 32 & 18 \\ \hline\end{array}$$

Which statement is supported by the data?
options: On average, people in City B are older than in City A.
The middle 50% of ages in City A are more consistent than in City B.
The oldest person in City B must be older than the oldest person in City A.
The mean age in both cities must be $32$.
questions.explanation: The IQR measures the spread of the middle 50% of the data. A smaller IQR (8 vs 18) indicates that the middle half of the population in City A has ages that are closer together (more consistent) than those in City B.

questions.question: A set of daily high temperatures for a city in July is found to have a very small standard deviation. Which of the following must be true about the distribution of these temperatures?
options: The temperatures were all very high.
The difference between the highest and lowest temperature was large.
The temperatures were all very close to the mean temperature.
The distribution is skewed to the right.
questions.explanation: Standard deviation is a measure of spread. A small standard deviation indicates that the data points in the distribution are clustered closely around the mean, regardless of whether that mean is a high or low value.

questions.question:
The table below shows the distribution of salaries at a large company.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|}\hline \text{Salary Range} & \text{Number of Employees} \\ \hline \$40{,}000-\$60{,}000 & \text{Large majority} \\ \hline \$500{,}000+ & \text{Very small number} \\ \hline\end{array}$$

Which measure of center would be most representative of a typical employee's salary?
options: Mean
Median
Range
Standard Deviation
questions.explanation: This distribution is skewed to the right due to the high-earning executives. In skewed distributions, the mean is pulled toward the outliers, while the median remains closer to the 'typical' values in the center of the data. Therefore, the median is the better measure of center here.

questions.question:
The table below compares test scores for two classes.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|}\hline \text{Class} & \text{Median Score} & \text{Range} \\ \hline \text{A} & 80 & 30 \\ \hline \text{B} & 80 & 15 \\ \hline\end{array}$$

Which of the following statements is a valid conclusion?
options: Class A has a more symmetric distribution than Class B.
The highest score in Class A was likely higher than the highest score in Class B.
The scores in Class B are more spread out than the scores in Class A.
The mean score of Class B must be higher than the mean score of Class A.
questions.explanation: Range is the difference between the maximum and minimum. Since Class A has a larger range (30) than Class B (15), its scores are more spread out. If both have the same median, it is highly likely that Class A's maximum value is higher and its minimum value is lower than those of Class B.

questions.question: A frequency curve for a set of data is perfectly symmetric. If the range of the data is $100$ and the mean is $150$, what is the value of the 50th percentile?
questions.explanation: In a perfectly symmetric distribution, the 50th percentile is exactly equal to the median, and the median is equal to the mean. Since the mean is 150, the 50th percentile (the median) is also 150.

questions.question: Which of the following distributions would likely have the highest standard deviation?
options: The heights of all $25$-year-old men in a specific city
The scores on a test where every student received an $85\%$
The ages of all residents in a retirement community
The annual rainfall totals for $50$ different countries around the world
questions.explanation: Standard deviation increases with the diversity and spread of the data. Heights of men of the same age and residents of a retirement community are relatively homogeneous groups. A test where everyone got the same score has a standard deviation of 0. Rainfall totals across 50 different countries would vary wildly based on climate and geography, creating the greatest spread.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent work! You can confidently interpret distribution shapes, compare measures of center and spread, and select the most appropriate statistic for skewed data.
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review missed questions and focus on remembering that the median is robust to outliers while the mean is not, and that IQR describes the middle 50% of data.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Remember: right skew = mean > median, left skew = mean < median, and symmetric = mean ≈ median. Standard deviation measures spread around the mean.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! Start by sketching distribution shapes to visualize skew, practice identifying which measure of center is most appropriate, and remember that range and IQR describe different aspects of spread.
